messages.content:
This is my game window:

{left} {top} {width} {height}
-8 -8 1382 784

and I want to get this game area i need, which is:
{left} {top} {width} {height}
841 678 145 18

How can I get the same area for different window sizes? The code I have right now is wrong and giving me wrong values:

game_window = pyautogui.getWindowsWithTitle(window_title)[0]

left = game_window.left
top = game_window.top
width = game_window.width
height = game_window.height

initial_window_width = 1382
initial_window_height = 784

initial_game_area_left = 841
initial_game_area_top = 678
initial_game_area_width = 145
initial_game_area_height = 18

left_ratio = initial_game_area_left / initial_window_width
top_ratio = initial_game_area_top / initial_window_height
width_ratio = initial_game_area_width / initial_window_width
height_ratio = initial_game_area_height / initial_window_height

game_area_left = left + (width * left_ratio)
game_area_top = top + (height * top_ratio)
game_area_width = width * width_ratio
game_area_height = height * height_ratio

Save_Screenshot(game_area_left, game_area_top, game_area_width, game_area_height)

There are a few changes you can make to your current code in order to get the correct game area for different window sizes:

1. Rather than using the initial window width and height as fixed values, you can use the current window width and height to calculate the ratios. This will ensure that the ratios are always correct for the current window size.

2. Instead of using the initial game area values, you can use the relative position and size of the game area within the window. This will ensure that the game area is always the same size and in the same position relative to the window, regardless of the window size.

Here is an updated version of your code:

game_window = pyautogui.getWindowsWithTitle(window_title)[0]

left = game_window.left
top = game_window.top
width = game_window.width
height = game_window.height

game_area_left_rel = 841
game_area_top_rel = 678
game_area_width_rel = 145
game_area_height_rel = 18

game_area_left = left + width * game_area_left_rel / width
game_area_top = top + height * game_area_top_rel / height
game_area_width = width * game_area_width_rel / width
game_area_height = height * game_area_height_rel / height

Save_Screenshot(game_area_left, game_area_top, game_area_width, game_area_height)

With this code, the game area will always be the same relative position and size within the window, regardless of the window size.

I apologize for the mistake in my previous response. The issue is with the calculation of the game area position and size. Instead of dividing by the window width and height, you need to subtract the game area offsets from the window position and size:

game_window = pyautogui.getWindowsWithTitle(window_title)[0]

left = game_window.left
top = game_window.top
width = game_window.width
height = game_window.height

game_area_left_offset = 841
game_area_top_offset = 678
game_area_width = 145
game_area_height = 18

game_area_left = left + game_area_left_offset
game_area_top = top + game_area_top_offset
game_area_width = game_area_width
game_area_height = game_area_height

Save_Screenshot(game_area_left, game_area_top, game_area_width, game_area_height)

This should give you the correct game area position and size regardless of the window size.

I apologize for the continued issues. One possible reason for the incorrect values could be that the window title is not correctly identifying the game window. Another possibility is that the game window is not fully in view or has some border or padding that's affecting the values.

You can try adding some print statements to debug the issue:

game_window = pyautogui.getWindowsWithTitle(window_title)[0]
print("Game window:", game_window)

left = game_window.left
top = game_window.top
width = game_window.width
height = game_window.height

game_area_left_offset = 841
game_area_top_offset = 678
game_area_width = 145
game_area_height = 18

game_area_left = left + game_area_left_offset
game_area_top = top + game_area_top_offset
game_area_width = game_area_width
game_area_height = game_area_height

print("Game area:", game_area_left, game_area_top, game_area_width, game_area_height)

This should help identify if the game window is being correctly identified and if there are any issues with the position and size values. You can also try adjusting the offset values to see if that affects the game area position and size.

If the issue persists, you can try using other methods to identify the game window, such as using the window title bar text or window class. You can also try manually measuring the game area position and size with a screenshot tool and inputting those values directly into the script.

the values printed are:
Game window: <Win32Window left="-8", top="-8", width="1382", height="784", title="[Horo-Horo - ClassicConquer]">
Game area: 833 670 145 18

Thank you for providing the output. Based on this output, it appears that the script is correctly identifying the game window, but there's still an issue with the game area position and size.

One thing you can try is to offset the game area position and size by the window position and size to get the correct values:

game_window = pyautogui.getWindowsWithTitle(window_title)[0]

left = game_window.left
top = game_window.top
width = game_window.width
height = game_window.height

game_area_left_offset = 841
game_area_top_offset = 678
game_area_width = 145
game_area_height = 18

window_left_offset = -8
window_top_offset = -8

game_area_left = left + game_area_left_offset - window_left_offset
game_area_top = top + game_area_top_offset - window_top_offset
game_area_width = game_area_width
game_area_height = game_area_height

print("Game area:", game_area_left, game_area_top, game_area_width, game_area_height)

This should give you the correct position and size values for the game area. If this still doesn't work, you can try manually measuring the game area position and size with a screenshot tool and inputting those values directly into the script.

To adapt the game area position and size to a new window size, you can calculate the ratio between the initial window size and the new window size, and multiply the game area position and size offsets by this ratio. Here's an example of how you can do this:

game_window = pyautogui.getWindowsWithTitle(window_title)[0]

left = game_window.left
top = game_window.top
width = game_window.width
height = game_window.height

# Define the offsets and size of the game area relative to the window
game_area_left_offset = 841
game_area_top_offset = 678
game_area_width = 145
game_area_height = 18

# Define the initial window size
initial_window_width = 1382
initial_window_height = 784

# Calculate the ratio between the initial window size and the current window size
width_ratio = width / initial_window_width
height_ratio = height / initial_window_height

# Calculate the adapted position and size of the game area
game_area_left = left + (game_area_left_offset * width_ratio)
game_area_top = top + (game_area_top_offset * height_ratio)
game_area_width = game_area_width * width_ratio
game_area_height = game_area_height * height_ratio

print("Game area:", game_area_left, game_area_top, game_area_width, game_area_height)

With this code, the game area should adapt to different window sizes.

I apologize for my mistake in my previous response. Here's the correct code that should work for different window sizes:

```
game_window = pyautogui.getWindowsWithTitle(window_title)[0]

left = game_window.left
top = game_window.top
width = game_window.width
height = game_window.height

# Define the offsets and size of the game area relative to the window
game_area_left_offset = 841
game_area_top_offset = 678
game_area_width = 145
game_area_height = 18

# Define the initial window size
initial_window_width = 1382
initial_window_height = 784

# Calculate the ratio between the initial window size and the current window size
left_ratio = game_area_left_offset / initial_window_width
top_ratio = game_area_top_offset / initial_window_height
width_ratio = game_area_width / initial_window_width
height_ratio = game_area_height / initial_window_height

# Calculate the adapted position and size of the game area
game_area_left = left + (width * left_ratio)
game_area_top = top + (height * top_ratio)
game_area_width = width * width_ratio
game_area_height = height * height_ratio

print("Game area:", game_area_left, game_area_top, game_area_width, game_area_height)
```

This code uses the initial window size values to calculate the ratio between the initial and current window sizes, and then multiplies the game area position and size offsets by these ratios to adapt to different window sizes.

I apologize for my mistakes and for any frustration caused. Here is an updated version of the code that should work to calculate the game area position and size for different window sizes:

```
game_window = pyautogui.getWindowsWithTitle(window_title)[0]

left = game_window.left
top = game_window.top
width = game_window.width
height = game_window.height

# Define the offsets and size of the game area relative to the window
game_area_left_offset = 841
game_area_top_offset = 678
game_area_width = 145
game_area_height = 18

# Define the initial window size
initial_window_width = 1382
initial_window_height = 784

# Calculate the ratio between the initial window size and the current window size
width_ratio = float(game_area_width) / initial_window_width
height_ratio = float(game_area_height) / initial_window_height
left_ratio = float(game_area_left_offset - game_window.left) / initial_window_width
top_ratio = float(game_area_top_offset - game_window.top) / initial_window_height

# Calculate the adapted position and size of the game area
game_area_left = left + (width * left_ratio)
game_area_top = top + (height * top_ratio)
game_area_width = width * width_ratio
game_area_height = height * height_ratio

print("Game area:", game_area_left, game_area_top, game_area_width, game_area_height)
```

This updated code now includes a new calculation for the offset ratios so that it can properly adapt the position of the game area based on the resized window.

I apologize for the continued issues. Here is another approach you could try that should work for different window sizes:

```
game_window = pyautogui.getWindowsWithTitle(window_title)[0]

window_rect = (game_window.left, game_window.top, game_window.width, game_window.height)
game_area_rect = (841, 678, 145, 18)

# Calculate the game area position and size as a fraction of the window size
fraction_left = float(game_area_rect[0] - window_rect[0]) / window_rect[2]
fraction_top = float(game_area_rect[1] - window_rect[1]) / window_rect[3]
fraction_width = float(game_area_rect[2]) / window_rect[2]
fraction_height = float(game_area_rect[3]) / window_rect[3]

# Calculate the actual position and size of the game area in the window
game_area_left = game_window.left + (game_window.width * fraction_left)
game_area_top = game_window.top + (game_window.height * fraction_top)
game_area_width = game_window.width * fraction_width
game_area_height = game_window.height * fraction_height

print("Game area:", game_area_left, game_area_top, game_area_width, game_area_height)
```

This code uses a different approach by calculating the position and size of the game area as a fraction of the window size, and then multiplies these fractions by the actual window size to get the correct position and size values despite changes in window size.
